text:
There once was a young girl named Amelia who loved nothing more than climbing trees.

One sunny afternoon, she discovered an especially big oak. With determination in her eyes, she climbed all the way up its branch. She grinned up at the stars.

Her feet hurt. But she loved everything else about being up there.

Then something caught her eye. It was a small creature. Something fluffy!

Amelia didn't know much about animals. But they sounded adorable.

She climbed down slowly. With careful steps, she approached the furry visitor.

It wasn't rabbit or mouse. It was something else.

Her nose twitched. Something sweet.

She reached out slowly. The squirrel chewed happily.

Eventually, Amelia gently lowered herself down.

They became fast friends.

Every day afterwards, Amelia would visit the big oak. She'd call the squirrel comrade. And they'd explore the woods together.

Their adventures were endless.

And sometimes, just sometimes?

She'd build them a tiny castle out of leaves.
